user_outcome: sworn's vendored Baton protocol is pinned to the v0.7.1 upstream tag: both embed roots carry the reconciled schemas (in_scope/out_of_scope as required spec-v1 fields, the published verifier-verdict-v1), the spec writer emits and the strict reader accepts the new fields, the quadrant enum gains 'quick' in place of 'chore', and acknowledged_by round-trips cleanly — the code half of sworn#48. The same pin also closes sworn#80: a track's worktree branch/path/state are no longer persisted, racy board.json fields — they're computed from (release, track-id) and git ancestry, so /implement-slice never writes to release-wt and /merge-track stays the sole write point.
rationale: sworn#48, rolled into this release by decision 2026-07-02. Grounding: the pin is currently v0.6.3 @ a5aca64 (internal/adopt/baton/VERSION); preconditions sworn#23 (tool-neutral source map) and sworn#24 (VERSION centralisation) are CLOSED. PRECONDITION: the upstream tag v0.7.1 does not exist yet — baton main sits at cd42ca1 (dc3b7cc added in_scope/out_of_scope AFTER the v0.7.0 tag; cd42ca1 fixed the role-prompt scope->user_outcome prose) and cd42ca1 is not yet pushed. Cut and push the v0.7.1 tag before this slice starts; the pin must reference a public tag per the repo's vendoring policy. Adoption points verified live: writer internal/spec/spec.go, strict reader internal/implement/spec_record.go; quadrant code internal/state/state.go:410,426 with fixtures in internal/state, internal/run/parallel_test.go, internal/router/router_test.go, cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go, cmd/sworn/regress_test.go. Transitional tolerance: Validate accepts BOTH quick and chore until S12 migrates the live records, then S12 tightens to quick-only — the track merges as one unit so the integration branch never carries the intermediate state. sworn#80 rolled in by decision 2026-07-03: two concurrent /implement-slice sessions (S02, S04) raced on the release-wt board.json write implement-slice makes when materialising a track's worktree, because tracks[].worktree_path/worktree_branch/state are modeled as persisted data even though track-mode.md's 'Naming, locked' table already makes branch/path a pure function of (release, track-id), and state is derivable via the same merge-base --is-ancestor check /merge-track's own idempotency gate already performs. This directly resolves the ONE board-v1 divergence baton#54 flagged that concerns track worktree binding (flattened worktree_path/worktree_branch vs upstream's nested worktree{path,branch}) — not by reshaping to match upstream, but by removing the fields as persisted data entirely, which moots the shape question for them. Explicitly out of scope: board-v1's remaining canonical-conformance gaps (additionalProperties strictness + id_token pattern, vertical_trace/target_version/integration_branch schema declarations) and proof-v1/journeys-v1/attestations-v1 conformance — baton#54 closed without actioning these; tracked fresh in baton#55.
in_scope: internal/adopt/baton/VERSION pin bump to the v0.7.1 tag (SHA + digest) and re-sync of that embed root from the tag

internal/baton/schemas re-sync (spec-v1 with required in_scope/out_of_scope; reconciled status/journeys/attestations; verifier-verdict-v1; board-v1 with worktree_path/worktree_branch/state removed from tracks[])
Quadrant enum code change: 'quick' replaces 'chore' as the low/low value, with transitional dual acceptance until S12
Spec writer (internal/spec) emits in_scope/out_of_scope; strict reader (internal/implement/spec_record.go) accepts and exposes them
acknowledged_by round-trip smoke test through writer + reader + schema validation
Test-fixture updates across internal/state, internal/run, internal/router, cmd/sworn

sworn#80: pure helper functions deriving track worktree branch (track/<release>/<track-id>) and path (the naming-convention path) from (release, track-id); track state (planned/in_progress/merged) derived from git ref existence + merge-base --is-ancestor against release-wt/<release>
internal/board (Oracle.readTrackInfos, ReadSliceStatus, render.go) computes branch/path/state via the new helpers instead of parsing them from board.json

internal/adopt/baton/rules/ command specs: implement-slice.md Step 0 no longer writes track worktree identity/state to release-wt's board.json on first dispatch; merge-track.md Step 5 no longer sets track state (derived) — /merge-track's own merge commit remains the sole release-wt write for a track
out_of_scope: board-v1's remaining canonical-conformance gaps beyond track worktree/state fields (additionalProperties strictness + id_token pattern, vertical_trace/target_version/integration_branch schema declarations) — tracked in baton#55 (successor to baton#54, which closed without actioning these)
verifier-verdict-v1 upstreaming decision, and vendoring the 4 un-vendored design/architecture schemas — same baton#55
Migrating the live docs/release records (S12 owns the data half)
Retry-on-conflict hardening for /merge-track's residual board.json write race (smaller surface once state is derived; separate follow-up)

effort_complexity.rationale: Was grind (known-shape vendor bump + enum rename, fixture sweep across five packages). Folding in sworn#80 raises complexity to high: the correct worktree-path derivation isn't track-mode.md's plainly-documented formula but the repo-local sibling-of-release-worktree logic worker.go already carries for a real prior incident (eval finding 3) — the implementer must find and preserve that nuance, not re-derive the naive convention. It also requires a cross-package consumer sweep (~12 files verified to read through internal/board's TrackInfo, not board.json directly) and coordinated baton-repo schema/command-spec edits landing before the v0.7.1 tag. Re-assess at design_review.

risks.description: The upstream v0.7.1 tag does not exist at slice start (cd42ca1 unpushed).
risks.mitigation: Named precondition: Brad cuts and pushes the tag first; the slice BLOCKs (dependent-on-human) rather than pinning an untagged SHA.

risks.description: The vendored spec-v1 flip to required in_scope/out_of_scope fails validation of historical records between S11 and S12.
risks.mitigation: S11 and S12 are sequential in one track and merge as a unit; no gate on the integration branch sees the intermediate state. Any in-track gate run between the slices is a known-transitional condition named in the journal.

risks.description: A tightened reader/schema regresses test fixtures in OTHER packages (the S05-strict-reader lesson).
risks.mitigation: The fixture sweep is in-scope and enumerated in touchpoints; the merge gate's full `go test ./...` is the backstop.

risks.description: Deriving track state/worktree identity instead of persisting it could silently expand into baton#54/#55's full board-v1 canonical-conformance scope (nested worktree object, additionalProperties strictness, vertical_trace/target_version/integration_branch), which this slice explicitly excludes.
risks.mitigation: out_of_scope names the excluded items and their tracking issue (baton#55) explicitly; AC-06/AC-07/AC-08 bound the change to exactly worktree_path/worktree_branch/state derivation, nothing else in board-v1's shape.

risks.description: ~12 other packages (internal/run, internal/scheduler, internal/router, internal/tui, internal/mcp, internal/account, internal/bench, cmd/sworn/merge.go, cmd/sworn/route.go) read TrackInfo.WorktreeBranch/WorktreePath. A naive fix that only changes what's parsed, without confirming every consumer reads through the struct internal/board populates (rather than re-parsing board.json independently), would silently break them.
risks.mitigation: Verified by grep + read: every consumer reads via the TrackInfo/Oracle struct internal/board assembles (a field re-exposure, e.g. internal/mcp/context.go:80,107), not by independently parsing board.json. Switching internal/board's population from JSON-parse to computed-value requires zero consumer changes, EXCEPT internal/scheduler/worker.go's defaultTrackWorktreePath (worker.go:600-621) — an existing fallback for when the board doesn't pin a path, itself added to fix a real incident (comment: 'the old default ... was identical for every repo, so a fired-repo run silently materialised on sworn's worktree tree — eval finding 3'). That repo-local (sibling-of-release-worktree) logic is the CORRECT derivation and must be the one internal/board's new helper uses — NOT track-mode.md's plainly-documented `$HOME/projects/<REPO_BASENAME>-worktrees/...` formula, which is the naive convention eval finding 3 already moved away from. Once internal/board always returns a populated, correct WorktreePath, worker.go's now-redundant fallback is deleted, not left as dead code. internal/mcp/tools_plan.go already independently derives WorktreeBranch as track/<release>/<track-id> (line 148) — confirms the branch-derivation half of this design is already a proven pattern; point it at the new shared helper instead of its own inline fmt.Sprintf for a single source of truth.

acceptance_criteria.text: internal/adopt/baton/VERSION SHALL pin baton-protocol v0.7.1 with the tag's upstream SHA and content digest, and both embed roots (internal/adopt/baton rules/docs, internal/baton/schemas) SHALL be re-synced byte-identical from that tag; `sworn doctor` SHALL report the new pin.

state.EffortComplexity SHALL treat 'quick' as the canonical low-effort/low-complexity quadrant (Quadrant() returns 'quick'; comments/docs updated) while Validate transitionally accepts 'chore' with a deprecation warning until S12 completes the data migration; grind/puzzle/epic are unchanged (TestQuadrantQuick covers canonical, transitional, and rejected values).

When the spec writer (internal/spec) emits a spec record, in_scope and out_of_scope SHALL be present (planner-supplied content or empty arrays), and the strict reader (internal/implement/spec_record.go) SHALL parse and expose both fields; a writer-to-reader round-trip test SHALL validate the record against the vendored v0.7.1 spec-v1 schema.

When a status record carrying acknowledged_by is written and re-read, the field SHALL round-trip unchanged and the record SHALL validate against the vendored slice-status schema (the sworn#48 item-4 smoke test).

`go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./internal/state/... ./internal/spec/... ./internal/implement/... ./internal/run/... ./internal/router/... ./internal/board/... ./internal/scheduler/... ./internal/mcp/... ./cmd/sworn/...` SHALL pass with all quadrant fixtures updated.

The vendored board-v1.json schema SHALL NOT declare `worktree_path`, `worktree_branch`, or `state` as properties of `tracks[]` entries (only `id`, `slices`, `depends_on` remain); internal/board SHALL NOT read or require these fields from board.json for any track.

When internal/board resolves a track's worktree branch, path, or state (Oracle.readTrackInfos, ReadSliceStatus, render.go), it SHALL compute the branch as track/<release>/<track-id>, the path as the sibling-of-release-worktree location internal/scheduler/worker.go's defaultTrackWorktreePath already derives (never the plain $HOME/projects/<repo>-worktrees formula that caused eval finding 3's cross-repo collision), and the state as: no such branch ref exists -> planned; branch exists and is not an ancestor of release-wt/<release> -> in_progress; branch is an ancestor of release-wt/<release> -> merged (the same merge-base --is-ancestor check cmd/sworn/merge.go already performs for its own gate). internal/scheduler/worker.go's now-redundant defaultTrackWorktreePath fallback SHALL be removed once internal/board always returns a populated path.

The vendored implement-slice.md and merge-track.md command specs (internal/adopt/baton/rules/) SHALL NOT instruct writing a track's worktree identity or state to release-wt's board.json. /merge-track's own merge commit (already required) remains the only release-wt write attributable to a track; /implement-slice makes none.
